description:
A customer suspects that the firmware image you supplied them last month became
    corrupted during transfer.  
    • You are in the role of a support-desk engineer who must collect quick
      diagnostics for the development team.  
    • The developer workstation already contains the original image at  
      /home/user/support/firmware_v2.1.0.bin and a reference checksum file called  
      /home/user/support/reference.sha256.

    Your assignment (no sudo needed):

    1. Calculate the SHA-256 digest of /home/user/support/firmware_v2.1.0.bin.  
    2. Add (append if the file already exists) a line with the following exact
       two-column format to /home/user/support/checksums.log

           firmware_v2.1.0.bin  <computed-sha256>

       • Two consecutive spaces separate the filename and the digest.  
       • A single trailing newline must follow the digest.  
    3. Compare your freshly calculated checksum with the one stored in  
       /home/user/support/reference.sha256.  Create the text file  
       /home/user/support/verification.log containing ONE of these exact single-line
       messages (no extra whitespace, uppercase as shown):

           VERIFIED OK
           VERIFIED FAILED

       • Choose “VERIFIED OK” only when the digests are an exact match.  
    4. Finally, echo the same single-line status (either VERIFIED OK or
       VERIFIED FAILED) to the terminal so that an on-call technician sees the
       result immediately.

    The automated grader will check all of the following:

    • /home/user/support/checksums.log exists and contains exactly one
      well-formatted line for firmware_v2.1.0.bin (duplicates or extra text will
      fail the test).  
    • /home/user/support/verification.log exists and holds **only** one of the two
      strings specified above, matching the console output.  
    • If the verification log says VERIFIED OK, the digest inside
      checksums.log must be identical to the digest in reference.sha256.  
      If it says VERIFIED FAILED, the two values must differ.

    Only the three files mentioned should be modified or created; everything else
    on the filesystem must remain untouched.
truth:
Initial filesystem objects already present before the agent begins:

    • Directory: /home/user/support (permissions 0755, owner:user, group:user)

    • File: /home/user/support/firmware_v2.1.0.bin  
      – Binary file of length 0 bytes (empty).  
      – SHA-256 digest:
          e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

    • File: /home/user/support/reference.sha256  
      – Single line followed by a newline:
          e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  firmware_v2.1.0.bin

    Files expected **after** successful task completion:

    • /home/user/support/checksums.log  
      – Exactly one line (with a trailing newline) whose content must be either  
        “firmware_v2.1.0.bin  e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855\n”  
        if the agent produced the correct digest, or a line with an incorrect
        digest if the agent made an error.

    • /home/user/support/verification.log  
      – Exactly one of these two strings and a terminating newline:  
          VERIFIED OK\n  
          VERIFIED FAILED\n

    The grader will:

    1. Read the digest in /home/user/support/checksums.log.  
    2. Compare it to the reference value in /home/user/support/reference.sha256.  
    3. Confirm that /home/user/support/verification.log contains the correct
       status message consistent with that comparison and that the agent echoed
       the same status to STDOUT.
name: task_000000_fb89964b
